mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to equity valuations, mechanically lifting S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 7.0 percent, labor market tightening accelerates wage growth expectations, which boosts corporate profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day forward returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE drops below 7.0, or the predictive Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in industrial production and a contraction in consumer credit availability signals a supply-constrained demand environment that compresses corporate margins, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative 60-day returns in more than 60% of triggered periods, or the interaction term in a multivariate regression yields a p-value greater than 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: In a housing market recovery phase, rising building permits act as a leading indicator for consumer wealth and durable goods spending, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain flat or negative when HOUST rises for three consecutive months, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 50% in a 12-month walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds a year-over-year growth rate of 3.0 percent, inflation expectations anchor higher, forcing equity risk premiums to expand and depressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are positive in more than 60% of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds the 3.0% threshold, or the signal's information coefficient decays to zero over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained month-over-month increase in total nonfarm payrolls signals expanding aggregate demand and corporate revenue growth, mechanically supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative forward returns in more than 55% of consecutive positive payroll months, or the rolling Rank IC remains below 0.02 across all test periods.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where real GDP growth accelerates while the federal funds rate remains below 0.5 percent indicates an accommodative monetary stance supporting real economic expansion, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances, or the interaction coefficient in a panel regression is statistically indistinguishable from zero (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: During a capacity utilization expansion, rising industrial production indices reflect tightening supply conditions that precede margin compression and equity drawdowns, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive when INDPRO accelerates as described, or the signal's directional accuracy falls below 45% in a 12-month out-of-sample test.
